raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need grep -R "foo" /system; count lines. Use grep -R -n? just count matches. Use grep -R "foo" /system | tee /dev/tty | wc -l? Actually output to console then number. Use grep -R "foo" /system | tee /dev/tty | wc -l. But tee duplicates. Simpler: grep -R "foo" /system; echo "Matches: $(grep -R -c "foo" /system)". But need recursive. Use -c counts per file. For total lines: grep -R -c "foo" /system | awk '{s+=$1} END{print s}'. So final command: grep -R "foo" /system; echo "Total matches: $(grep -R -c "foo" /system | awk '{s+=$1} END{print s}')". Provide one line.
